assertions.title: Read-only mode allows viewing but blocks all mutating controls

steps.action: Run `thread_list_page_test.dart` case `shows loading then populated thread rows with status and context` with the harness approval API fixed to `AccessMode.readOnly`
steps.expected: Read-only users can still browse thread rows and context
steps.observed: The widget test passed and rendered populated thread rows with status/context in the read-only harness.

steps.action: Run `thread_detail_page_test.dart` case `changing access mode from settings immediately gates turn and git controls`
steps.expected: Switching to read-only disables mutating controls on an already open thread surface
steps.observed: The widget test passed and disabled turn submit plus git pull after the runtime mode changed to read-only.

evidence.consoleErrors: not applicable on the Flutter widget-test surface
evidence.network: not applicable; the widget harness uses fake bridge APIs

assertions.issues: Blocked on evidence sufficiency: the assigned widget surface proves read-only thread browsing and some control gating, but it does not directly exercise approvals-state visibility or the full read-only mutation matrix (approve/reject/push/branch switch/live-output visibility) required by the assertion.

assertions.title: Control-with-approvals mode permits normal control but gates dangerous actions

steps.action: Run `thread_detail_page_test.dart` case `idle composer starts turn and transitions to active controls` against a thread fixture whose access mode is `controlWithApprovals`
steps.expected: Starting a turn remains available in control-with-approvals mode
steps.observed: The widget test passed; submitting the composer transitioned the UI to `Steer turn`, exposed interrupt, and showed `Running`.

steps.action: Run `thread_detail_page_test.dart` case `active composer steers and interrupt stops the active turn` against a thread fixture whose access mode is `controlWithApprovals`
steps.expected: Steer and interrupt controls remain available in control-with-approvals mode
steps.observed: The widget test passed; steer submitted successfully and interrupt returned the thread to `Interrupted`.

steps.action: Run `approvals_queue_page_test.dart` case `lower-permission mode keeps approve/reject disabled` with `AccessMode.controlWithApprovals`
steps.expected: Dangerous approval actions stay gated instead of executing directly
steps.observed: The widget test passed; approve/reject were rendered disabled and the UI explained they are available only in full-control mode.

evidence.consoleErrors: not applicable on the Flutter widget-test surface
evidence.network: not applicable; the widget harness uses fake bridge APIs

assertions.title: Full-control mode exposes actionable approval controls on mobile

steps.action: Run `approvals_queue_page_test.dart` case `full-control mode can approve pending approvals`
steps.expected: Full-control mode exposes actionable approval controls and can resolve a pending approval
steps.observed: The widget test passed; tapping approve resolved the approval and surfaced approved/already-resolved feedback.

steps.action: Run `approvals_queue_page_test.dart` case `lower-permission mode keeps approve/reject disabled`
steps.expected: Lower-permission modes can show approval state without actionable approval buttons
steps.observed: The widget test passed; approve/reject controls were present but disabled with an explanatory message.

evidence.consoleErrors: not applicable on the Flutter widget-test surface
evidence.network: not applicable; the widget harness uses fake bridge APIs

assertions.title: Mobile unpair/reset clears trust locally and requires fresh pairing

steps.action: Run `pairing_flow_test.dart` case `unpairing from settings clears local trust and returns to unpaired UI`
steps.expected: Unpairing from settings clears local trust/session state and returns to the unpaired flow
steps.observed: The widget test passed; the app returned to `Pair your phone to this Mac`, secure-store trust/session secrets became null, and revokeTrust was called once.

evidence.consoleErrors: not applicable on the Flutter widget-test surface
evidence.network: not applicable; the widget harness uses fake bridge APIs

assertions.title: Settings exposes paired bridge info, access mode, and connection state

steps.expected: Settings shows paired bridge identity, connection state, access mode, and runtime preferences
steps.observed: The widget test passed and rendered `Paired bridge`, `Bridge id: bridge-a1`, `Connection: Connected`, `Control-with-approvals mode`, and both notification toggles.

evidence.consoleErrors: not applicable on the Flutter widget-test surface
evidence.network: not applicable; the widget harness uses fake bridge APIs

assertions.title: Access mode can be changed from settings or device management with immediate visible effect

steps.action: Run `settings_page_test.dart` case `settings access mode switch updates bridge policy`
steps.expected: Changing access mode from settings updates the bridge policy for the paired phone
steps.observed: The widget test passed; selecting `Read-only` produced a `setAccessMode` call for `phone-a1`.

steps.action: Run `thread_detail_page_test.dart` case `changing access mode from settings immediately gates turn and git controls`
steps.expected: An already open thread surface immediately reflects the new access mode
steps.observed: The widget test passed; the turn submit and git pull buttons became disabled immediately after the runtime mode changed to read-only.

evidence.consoleErrors: not applicable on the Flutter widget-test surface
evidence.network: not applicable; the widget harness uses fake bridge APIs, but the fake settings API recorded the mode-change request

assertions.title: Notification preference changes affect runtime delivery behavior

steps.expected: Eligible notifications surface while the user is off thread routes when delivery is enabled
steps.observed: The widget test passed and surfaced an approval notification banner while the user was on Device settings rather than a thread route.

steps.action: Run `settings_page_test.dart` case `settings access mode switch updates bridge policy` and toggle the approval notifications switch within that harness
steps.expected: The runtime preference can be changed from settings
steps.observed: The widget test passed and the approval notifications switch flipped off in the settings UI.

evidence.consoleErrors: not applicable on the Flutter widget-test surface
evidence.network: not applicable; the widget harness uses fake bridge APIs

assertions.issues: Blocked on evidence sufficiency: the assigned widget surface proves enabled delivery and a settings toggle, but it does not directly prove user-visible suppression after disabling notifications or that approvals/thread access remain unaffected while disabled. The repo has controller-level tests for those cases, but they are not user-surface evidence.

assertions.title: Recent security events show actor, target, and outcome for sensitive actions

steps.expected: Settings surfaces recent security events with actor, target, and outcome
steps.observed: The widget test passed and rendered `Actor: mobile-settings`, `Target: policy.access_mode`, and `set_access_mode • allowed`.

steps.action: Run `settings_page_test.dart` case `security events render newest-first before truncation`
steps.expected: Recent security events are ordered newest-first before truncation
steps.observed: The widget test passed and showed `evt-security-10`/`evt-security-9` while older events were truncated.

evidence.consoleErrors: not applicable on the Flutter widget-test surface
evidence.network: not applicable; the widget harness uses fake bridge APIs

assertions.title: Access mode changes take effect across turn controls, approvals, and git actions without reinstall or re-pair

steps.action: Run `thread_detail_page_test.dart` case `changing access mode from settings immediately gates turn and git controls`
steps.expected: An already open thread surface immediately reflects access-mode changes for turn and git actions
steps.observed: The widget test passed and immediately disabled turn submit plus git pull after switching to read-only.

steps.action: Run approval-mode coverage with `approvals_queue_page_test.dart` cases for full-control and lower-permission approval actions
steps.expected: Approval affordances should reflect policy by mode
steps.observed: Separate widget tests passed showing full-control approval actions enabled and lower-permission approval actions disabled.

assertions.issues: Blocked on evidence sufficiency: the assigned widget surface proves immediate turn/git gating and separate mode-dependent approval states, but it does not directly exercise an already open approvals surface changing policy in-place or any in-flight approval attempt during a runtime mode switch.

blockers.description: The assigned `flutter-mobile-widget-tests` surface does not include a widget case that fully exercises the entire read-only mutation matrix and approvals-state visibility required by VAL-PAIR-007.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Searched the existing Flutter widget harnesses for read-only, approval, and settings coverage and used the closest matching thread-list and thread-detail cases; no fuller user-surface case exists in the assigned harnesses.

blockers.description: Notification suppression-after-disable and cross-surface approval-mode refresh are only covered outside the user-surface widget harness (for example in controller tests), so the assigned surface cannot fully satisfy VAL-SETTINGS-004 and VAL-CROSS-004 from user-visible evidence alone.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Searched the existing Flutter widget harnesses for disabled-notification and runtime approval-mode-switch cases; only enabled-delivery and separate per-mode approval cases are present.

summary: Tested 9 assertions: 6 passed, 0 failed, 3 blocked (VAL-PAIR-007, VAL-SETTINGS-004, VAL-CROSS-004) due to insufficient user-surface widget evidence for the full contract breadth.
